data.information: Free N95 masks are available, per Ouray County Public Heath.
Free N95 masks are available, per Ouray County Public Heath.

Livestock sheltering is available for those impacted by the Gold Mountain Fire in Ouray County. If you need shelter for livestock, please call the hotline at 970-626-5484 for registration prior to arrival.

Shelter Location:

Montrose County Fairgrounds
1036 North 7th Montrose, CO

Please note the following before bringing animals to the shelter:

• Water your animals before transport.

• Bring feed if you are able.

• All animals will be inspected by a veterinarian upon arrival.

• Owners are responsible for feeding, watering, exercising, and maintaining their animals while they are sheltered.

Instructions:

  • Enter the Montrose County Fairgrounds via the San Juan entrance north of Main Street.

  • Approach the fenced area where the white rows of permanent horse stalls are located, southwest from the San Juan entrance.

  • Enter the fenced area through the gate and continue through the horse stall area to the last three sets of stalls. Barns D, E and I have been designated for sheltering evacuated animals.
FREE KN-95 masks are available at the following locations due to Public Health being closed today.
FREE KN-95 masks are available at the following locations due to Public Health being closed today.
FREE KN-95 masks are available at the following locations due to Public Health being closed today.
The Shelter and Evacuation Center is OPEN at the Ridgway Secondary School at 1200 Green Street, per the Ouray County Sheriff's Office.

If you have been evacuated and need a safe place for your small animals, please reach out to Leadville/Lake County Animal Shelter.  They are open at 1000AM Monday June 29th,  719-486-8591.

Displaced residents may report to the Family Assistance Center at Colorado Mountain College Gym - per updated information from Lake County Office of Emergency Management on Facebook.

A credentialing process has begun to confirm evacuee status at Colorado Mountain College Leadville (CMC) Gym, in the Climax Building (901 US 24 Leadville CO).

data.information:
As of 11:20 a.m. The large animal/livestock evacuation center is being re-located to the Colorado State Fairgrounds, enter at Gate 7 ONLY off Small Ave.

The site at 4 Bar S will be closing.

-Per Pueblo County Sheriff's Office on Facebook.
An Evacuation Center is open to people and animals in Fremont County at Pathfinder Park (6655 CO-115, Florence, CO 81226), per Custer County Sheriff's Office on Facebook.

An evacuation center is open in Custer County at Lange Hall, 312 S. 3rd Street, Westcliffe, per the Pueblo County Emergency Status Board.

Small animals accepted, per the Custer County Sheriff's Office.
An evacuation center has been set up and is open in Pueblo County at 1650 Cooper Place. Household pets are being accepted. Via Pueblo County Sheriff's Office.

A Disaster Assistance Center is available at the Pueblo Academy of Arts (formerly Pitts Middle School), 29 Lehigh Ave, Pueblo, CO 81005, for Pueblo County and Custer County residents who have been evacuated or impacted by the Aspen Acres Fire.

The Disaster Assistance Center (DAC) is a one-stop resource center set up to assist survivors with the recovery process.

Among the services that will be available at the center include victims assistance, United Way of Southern Colorado, food, Pueblo Department of Public Health and Environment, behavioral health, the Department of Human Services, Red Cross, agriculture support and private insurance.

Individuals will be required to have proof of residency to enter the DAC, which can include photo ID with address, a piece of mail or other official document with address. The entrance to the DAC will be on the east side of the school, enter from the parking lot off of Colgate Lane.

The DAC will open the following days and times:

Thursday, July 2 from 1 to 5 p.m.
Friday, July 3 from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m.
Saturday, July 4, from 8 a.m. to 12:00 p.m.
Sunday, July 5 – CLOSED
Monday, July 6 – from 1 to 5 p.m.

Per the Pueblo County Sheriff's Office on Facebook.
